title: Penducella hippocrepica
intro: Penducella hippocrepica, also known as Horse Apple, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Penducella hippocrepica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of herbal tinctures and capsules.

Penducella hippocrepica is native to South Africa, where it grows in a variety of habitats.
